Fear and Greed is at
23
(Fear)
and BTC is near
$65K
this morning. The Fed wraps its two-day meeting today, with a
rate decision arriving this afternoon. Markets are watching
closely.

While traders wait for the Fed, Wall Street is not pausing.
BlackRock just launched a Bitcoin yield ETF. State Street entered
the stablecoin reserves race. Coinbase opened stock transfers to
its platform. Ripple backed Africa's largest fintech. The
infrastructure layer keeps expanding regardless of where rates
land today.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Coinbase Just Became an Everything Exchange

Coinbase announced on June 16 that US users can now transfer
existing stock portfolios onto the platform without selling their
holdings. Once transferred, stocks sit alongside crypto in a
single account.

Zero-commission trading, fractional shares, and TradingView
charting tools are included in the offering.

What does Coinbase's head of financial services say customers
want from a single account?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/zzUKToemUSWNkjdHkpQHHQ~~/AAQRxRA~/ybw7VHj4dXzG7M9kKEvdUyT-stct_zUpjAFMmlJjpvQw6y0Pia_eweLhznzPQd5VZYW44Ekvo0LFwGL_duq3w1YMqNxiCX7FjpE2oynGGoWiuDnPq4b74zToDz78lXzXLyRDti7tcQdcvMRKqAnfG5ITqGJ8zibVUQTJmkt71Co~ )

fund on June 16 specifically d

74666.subject: By Invitation: Vladimir Putin is losing his grip on Russia
74666.body:
The EconomistвҖҷs week ahead

РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: The Economist


РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: The Economist

    
    
    РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°Р№СӮРө СҶСҺ СҒСӮР°СӮСӮСҺ РҪР° LinkedIn, СүРҫРұ РҝСҖРёС”РҙРҪР°СӮРёСҒСҸ РҙРҫ РҫРұРіРҫРІРҫСҖРөРҪРҪСҸ
РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°СӮРё РҪР° LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/invitation-vladimir-putin-losing-his-grip-russia-the-economist-inrge?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B1dLwwkPERQ%2BTq56zGNPGdQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1W5eKGapTf6Yk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqhz59eh~k4-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqhz59eh-k4
  
Sign up for free here to receive this newsletter in your inbox every Saturday. YouвҖҷll also get our weekly newsletter with an exclusive introduction from our editor-in-chief. IвҖҷm not alone in thinking I may have the best job at The Economist. Running By Invitation, our guest-essays section, is certainly one of the most varied. Among our authors this year have been ArgentinaвҖҷs chainsaw-wielding president, EuropeвҖҷs вҖңAnti-TrumpвҖқ (aka SpainвҖҷs prime minister, Pedro SГЎnchez), two Nobel laureates, and a founder of IranвҖҷs Revolutionary Guards. These contributors are, mostly, a delight to deal with (no clues as to which of the above called one of my questions about their copy вҖңmoronicвҖқ). Names, names, names. And then there was the author with no name: the вҖңformer senior official in the Russian governmentвҖқ who penned a stinging take on Vladimir PutinвҖҷs RussiaвҖ”our most popular op-ed of 2026 so far. Readers are understandably hungry for informed glimpses into a strategically important regime that keeps a tight lid on information. Anonymity is rare on By Invitation: this was the first such piece since I took over more than three years ago. But sometimes itвҖҷs necessary. Mr Putin is not one to invite his domestic critics over to the Kremlin for a cup of teaвҖ”unless, perhaps, itвҖҷs laced with polonium. Matthew Valencia, By Invitation Editor EditorвҖҷs picks Vladimir Putin is losing his grip on Russia His every move to preserve power accelerates decay, writes a former senior official in the Russian government Javier Milei: Rein in regulators, not big companies ArgentinaвҖҷs president and Federico Sturzenegger, his deregulation minister, call for a radical rethink on government meddling Pedro SГЎnchez: No to war It is naive to think that escalating conflict in Iran will lead to anything good, writes SpainвҖҷs prime minister IranвҖҷs Revolutionary Guards wonвҖҷt defend the regime to the last man Mohsen Sazegara, one of the forceвҖҷs founders, believes its various units are more divided than they appear The IPO wave will enshrine the AI godsвҖҷ control over the future Looming offerings by SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic are potentially perilous experiments in corporate governance, writes Gill Whitehead Go beyond the headlines Get unlimited digital access to The Economist by subscribing today.
РҹСҖРҫРҙРҫРІР¶РёСӮРё СҮРёСӮР°СӮРё РҪР° LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/puls

74663.subject: Adyen and Stripe push deeper into Agentic Commerce after OpenAIвҖҷs checkout failure hands Google the protocol race вҖҰ
74663.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/fintechpulse1090

Hey Everyone,
Good morning & happy Wednesday! Today weвҖҷre diving into payments giant Adyen, which is now pushing deeper into Agentic Commerce after OpenAIвҖҷs checkout failure (what Adyen Agentic is all about, how it compares with StripeвҖҷs Agentic AI initiatives [ https://substack.com/redirect/cd73a595-a4df-4d94-a342-fb6311a7bfcd?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], & whatвҖҷs one thing nobody talks about here + bonus dives into GoogleвҖҷs bid to become the ultimate OS for all e-com [ https://substack.com/redirect/9144ea55-481a-4d6d-9c3b-fe4b38937749?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], Visa & MastercardвҖҷs AI challenges [ https://substack.com/redirect/30cbfdef-101d-46b3-9ff0-e50c7a6e2e65?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] & how to build your first AI Agent from scratch [ https://substack.com/redirect/139feb48-3247-4a6f-88dd-2ccc7ff78f85?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside), and Robinhood, which just laid of 10% of its employees without mentioning AI even once (what does that tell us, why it matters, & what it means for the future of fintech + bonus deep dive into Robinhood & CoinbaseвҖҷs latest financials [ https://substack.com/redirect/2f44d8df-0626-4663-a10b-fe613dadd56c?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] and why now itвҖҷs the perfect time to productize yourself [ https://substack.com/redirect/429acfd5-8582-4242-97cb-75846f0b1678?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside). So letвҖҷs jump straight into the interesting stuff рҹҢ¶пёҸ
Adyen and Stripe push deeper into Agentic Commerce after OpenAIвҖҷs checkout failure hands Google the protocol race рҹӨ–рҹ’і
The news рҹ—һпёҸ Two of the worldвҖҷs largest payment processors just escalated their agentic commerce bets within days of each other. Adyen [ https://substack.com/redirect/09edcb73-6692-45fa-97ca-30362fa58563?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] launched a full API suite for AI agent transactions. Stripe [ https://substack.com/redirect/b70fcbf3-fe0e-4e15-b00e-f97dda4531c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] expanded its own infrastructure through AmazonвҖҷs AWS.
Both moves come three months after OpenAIвҖҷs Instant Checkout collapsed (Walmart saw 3x worse conversion rates; fewer than 30 Shopify merchants ever went live) and weeks after Stripe joined GoogleвҖҷs Universal Commerce Protocol [ https://substack.com/redirect/136ace61-8ca6-4efc-b082-05c2f338ffa5?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], a quiet concession that the standard it co-built with OpenAI lost the race.
But the most telling detail is this - neither can point to real transaction volume from the channel theyвҖҷre building for рҹ‘Җ
So letвҖҷs take a closer look at this, understand why it matters, and wh
